title: LLM-assist extractor adapter (funded, budget-capped, review-only output)
context: For harder eligibility prose the project adds an LLM-assist extractor behind a vendor-neutral adapter, keeping the agent-neutral core free of vendor logic. Building the adapter is donated code work; at runtime the extraction itself runs via packages/runner on the funded lane with a hard per-trial budget cap. LLM output is never auto-merged and always enters the human-review queue.
objective: Build an LLM-assist extractor adapter behind the provider interface that runs only via packages/runner under a hard fundedBudgetUsd per-trial cap and routes all output to human review.
acceptanceCriteria: Adapter runs only via packages/runner with a hard fundedBudgetUsd per-trial cap; never exceeds the cap.
LLM output is never auto-merged; it always enters the human-review queue.
No secrets, keys, or tokens appear in logs, receipts, or output.
Adapter lives behind the vendor-neutral provider interface; the agent-neutral core stays vendor-neutral per CLAUDE.md.
